title: Shinjis Visit to Gehirn and the Sandbox Sequence
display_title: Shinjis Visit to Gehirn and the Sandbox Sequence
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's Visit to Gehirn and the Sandbox Sequence             Quote Post by Zaque » Thu Aug 21, 2008 12:45 am Just a verification question to those knowledgeable (sorry if this was asked before, I tried searching a little bit but didn't find anything). So, I was looking at the Timeline for the series, specifically in 2010 when Rei I is introduced, but I didn't find anything about Shinji during this time. Now, in Death, there is a screen caption that reads (at least the subtitles from my version say this): 5 years later she and he met again (referring, I can only assume to be Rei and Shinji). I would guess that this would mean that Shinji was at Gehirn at least sometime from the creation of Rei I to at the death of her (by Naoko). Then, looking at the Sandbox Sequence I'm wondering if it was during this visit to Gehirn that Shinji was also when Shinji was a "subject" to this sequence. And that Shinji was one of Misato's first "assignments" before she was shipped off to Germany. And also that nerv was built like a pyramid because Gendo thought it'd be cool or something based off of the results of this "experiment". I didn't see any mention of this in the sandbox sequence either, nor the explanation of Shinji's line "I thought that if I came here, I could find something" to refer to him visiting Gehirn. Now sure, the scene may be symbolic, but I'm thinking that it happened in the past during this visit. Anyway, I was wondering if someone could verify these musings for me, or tell me if this is just old news. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:29 am There's no indication that Misato was in Tokyo-3 before being in Germany and I'd assume that the Nerv pyramid was the same housing for Gehirn (which would mean it was built before Shinji was there the first time). I can't recall what exactly the "she and he met again" was referring to, I know it's been brought up before, maybe at ANF. Yeah, it was at ANF, you can see it here , although it doesn't say all that much. Like I said in my analysis, it's possible that it's a flashback but given the surreal bent to the entire sequence (stage, theatre lighting, the perfect symmetry), it could be entirely contrived by Shinji. This isn't to say that Shinji wasn't at some point a kid playing in a sandbox and had a similar experience with 2 other kids, but the important thing in that sequence isn't whether that happened before or where it happened, but the symbolism and his interactions.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Thu Aug 21, 2008 9:23 am Thanks for the link, it clarified that the translation of the text was correct. Now, this would be something that was told directly to the audience (and survived the various reedits of Death), so I think it means what it says. And from the scenes just prior and the scene just after this text, the only conclusion is that Rei and Shinji met before. As for Misato, well I don't know the details, but she joined Gehirn, right? which means that she should have at least been on site at least at some point to "apply", get her ID and other stuff, besides, she would have no reason to be in Germany before joining Gehirn anyway. As for the pyramid, I'm not sure, it may have been built as Gehirn, but I can't recall any scenes for that at the moment. As for the flashback part, I definitely agree that it was one. Moreover, I wonder if it was Shinji's teacher who suggested that he visit Gehirn. (it seems to me he was the one who started him on cello anyway, given he was 5 at the time and he was sent away when he was 3). This would also mean that Shinji was 9 during the Sandbox Sequence if everything is to fit right. As for the sandbox sequence's contrived nature, there's no doubt that there is that feeling to it (those creepy kids). What I'm trying to say here is that those lines might also have a more literal meaning to them. Wouldn't that be important?

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:23 pm I thought it was Yui that brought Shinji along to see her get sucked into Sho's core.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:26 pm swamp147 wrote: I thought it was Yui that brought Shinji along to see her get sucked into Sho's core.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 21, 2008 2:17 pm Zaque wrote: What I'm trying to say here is that those lines might also have a more literal meaning to them. Wouldn't that be important?

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Fri Aug 22, 2008 12:23 am Good link, I'll look more into it when I have the time, I just returned to a "school" schedule. What I wrote earlier were my "initial thoughts" regarding the eyecatch, and they seemed pretty straightforward at the time, and kind of still do. (who started Shinji on cello? his teacher. why did Shinji agree? IMO, perhaps he thought that he could find something. who might have prompted Shinji to visit his father at Gehirn in 2010 (if this happened anyway)? IMO, probably his teacher. why did Shinji decide to listen? IMO, perhaps he thought he could find something. And being "summoned" is different than being "told to", although I'll bet his teacher had something to do with Shinji going to Nerv anyway). Ornette wrote: We do know that he's been to Yui's grave since the CE, it's mentioned that the last meeting between Gendo and Shinji was 3 years ago, and in episode 15, Shinji says it was 3 years ago since they were both at Yui's grave. But I don't see the connection between that line and visiting his mother's grave with his father.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sat Aug 23, 2008 1:21 am Double post separated by time. It's a bit long, but it goes through each step of my thoughts leading to my conclusions. All right, I read that other thread, and not much more at the moment, and there was actually the comment that his teacher recommended him the cello regarding the line: "It's just like when I started the cello." Anyway, I'll start at the beginning of where I made these links. So, with the eyecatch: "5 years later-- she and he met again", along with the scenes that go with it, I'm guessing it's safe to assume that Shinji and Rei had met sometime in 2010. Okay, the only times we see Rei (I) in 2010 is when Gendo introduced her to Naoko, and then the scene when she was choked to death. From this, I think it is safe to assume that if Shinji and Rei, did infact meet in 2010 at some point in time, it would be between the time Rei (I) was "born"/created, and the time that Rei (I) was killed off by Naoko. If this is true, then the place where Rei I was at during this time would have been at Gehirn. I don't know where Gendo lived at this time, or where Shinji's teacher lived at this time, perhaps the same city, perhaps not. I don't know if the meeting between Rei and Shinji would have been at Gehirn, Gendo's dwelling, or Shinji's teacher's dwelling. However, it would make most sense if Rei I was kept at Gehirn under special care of the staff as she was a special subject. (I doubt that Shinji would visit Gendo at his place (if Gendo even left the lab), and even more so Gendo taking time to visit Shinji at Shinji's teacher's place). (This later might also tie into the line: "I thought that if I came here , I could find something...", here being someplace other than where Shinji lived at the time). From all this, I gather that Shinji went to Gehirn sometime in 2010 (Perhaps at the suggestion of his teacher, i might add, why else would he?). Now we get into the sandbox sequence. One of the things I learned was that the mother figure in the sequence WAS Misato. Around 2010 was also the time Misato joined Gehirn. She later was sent to germany for some reason or another (Asuka I think), but before that, she probably worked on site for at least a while. While this may not be too important on it's own, if indeed the sandbox sequence "really happened" the only time Misato would be there is sometime around 2010 to when she left (which apparently is in the same year). This means that there is only this window of time when the sandbox sequence "could have happened" if it was a "real" flashback. (This would also mean that Shinji was about 9 years old at the time of the sequence. I've compared the picture of Shinji at the contact experiment to this time, and Shinji does look "older" but that may also be my imagination. :P) Something I didn't catch when I first wrote about this was the figure at the scaffolding who looks and then turns away. While in the interpretation on the wiki page, it states that this is Shinji, I don't think that this is the case, I don't think Shinji left the sandbox the entire time of the sequence. He doesn't follow the children as they go away, so there is no reason in my mind that after they had left, he would go look. My initial thoughts on who the figure was, is Gendo. This partially has to do with how i had viewed the scene from earlier (my comment on Gendo building Nerv as a pyramid because that is what Shinji was building). So far, it all seems to fit. Now, back on those lines, I think that they may have a double meaning, the first, like was mentioned in the wiki talks about how Shinji views his current situation (well, why he came to nerv in the first place), but also it may refer to the actual sandbox sequence and the visit to Gehirn that he would have made to have met Rei I. Also, I noticed that in the other thread the line "It's just like when I started the cello" referred to why Shinji continued to pilot eva. I think that this statement is off because it doesn't have anything to do with "starting" anything. when Shinji "started" his cello, it was because his teacher recommended that he start it. Similarly, my thought is that his visit to Gehirn was the result of a recommendation from his teacher. Similarly (although different), he was summoned to nerv right? well he could also not have gone, but perhaps it was his teacher who also recommended that he go that time too. I remember Shinji saying something along the lines of "this is dumb, i shouldn't have come" during ep01 before he actually got to Nerv. This to me kinda implies that he had his reservations about coming, but was pushed (by his teacher) to go, for whatever reason. On to the line: "I thought that if I came here, I could find something...", again, it may have a double meaning (well triple), the first is about the cello. Perhaps Shinji thought that he could find something (what? who know? acknowledgement perhaps?) when he started the cello. the next meaning would be going to Nerv as we see at the beginning of the series, perhaps he went there for the same reasons (I would comment a little more about how after Gendo praises him, he feels happy, but I don't recall that all too well at the moment). The third meaning would then again apply to the scene at hand, the sandbox sequence. If he actually went to Gehirn at the recommendation of his teacher, this line would also refer to his reasons why he decided to go to Gehirn, perhaps Shinji thought he could find something (acknowledgement again). Well, if he did, we would guess that he at least found Rei (I), maybe just before she got "lost", who knows. Now for the last step, if we further add to this that the sandbox sequence was a setup (and it sure looked like one at that) that was created by Gehirn (probably just Gendo though) and executed by the newly hired Misato, everything just seems to fall right into place. It all fits is what i'm saying. Sure, the sandbox sequence has some definite symbolic value, but what I just wrote here would imply that if it were a "real" flashback, perhaps a faint one (graininess of picture), this would pretty much be the only logical explaination of it's occurance (although there may be slight variants of this, the general idea would remain the same: Shinji visits Gehirn, and Shinji was put through the Sandbox Sequence at 9 years of age). there might be other logical explainations, but I think that this would be at least the most straitforward one. something I don't know, and don't have the patience to scrounge through the series for: How Gehirn looked before Nerv was built. From what I recall though, the pyramid did seem to exist prior to 2010, but the labs at Gehirn may have been above ground, hence the "movement" of the Magi to Nerv during the transistion, I'm not sure. Anyway, that's what I think about how this aspect of eva connects, please point to anything wrong with it, or any changes or comments on it's validity and plausibility. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Aug 23, 2008 2:50 am Zaque wrote: Okay, the only times we see Rei (I) in 2010 is when Gendo introduced her to Naoko, and then the scene when she was choked to death. From this, I think it is safe to assume that if Shinji and Rei, did infact meet in 2010 at some point in time, it would be between the time Rei (I) was "born"/created, and the time that Rei (I) was killed off by Naoko.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sat Aug 23, 2008 9:40 am Ornette wrote: The only indication that this ever happened was in Death, which Anno didn't even have any involvement in.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sat Aug 23, 2008 3:19 pm Ornette wrote: Zaque wrote: Okay, the only times we see Rei (I) in 2010 is when Gendo introduced her to Naoko, and then the scene when she was choked to death. From this, I think it is safe to assume that if Shinji and Rei, did infact meet in 2010 at some point in time, it would be between the time Rei (I) was "born"/created, and the time that Rei (I) was killed off by Naoko.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Aug 23, 2008 3:32 pm Death was sort of adhoc'ed in because the theatrical release of EoE was behind schedule and wasn't going to make it's announced release date in time. So the production staff decided to make it 2 movies, and Tsurumaki put together the clipshow and the few new bits of animation which comprised of Death while the rest of the production team worked on finishing EoE. Death was then released along with an unfinished version of AIR (see here for the differences between Rebirth and EoE) as a teaser for the upcoming EoE. I still think there are more interpretations to that line of dialogue about finding something and the cello, I'm just not seeing it referring to an unverified visit to Gehirn in 2010.
